narrative.headline: 2022 Jeep Gladiator: steering and electrical lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 112 complaints with NHTSA. Steering is the most reported area at 29%, with the electrical system close behind at 28%. No deaths are reported. Fire shows up across several areas: 8 electrical reports, 7 engine reports, and 3 steering reports mention a fire, so the electrical and engine systems are worth a close look.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 33 reports (29%), with 3 fires, 1 crash, and 1 injury. Test the steering feel carefully on a drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 31 reports (28%), and the highest fire count of any area at 8, plus 1 injury. The fire count makes this worth checking closely.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 20 reports (18%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. A recurring complaint area without serious outcomes in the data.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 19 reports (17%), including 7 fires and 2 injuries. The high fire count makes the engine worth a careful look.

issues.title: Suspension
issues.blurb: 18 reports (16%), tied to 1 crash and no fires or injuries. The smallest of the top five complaint areas.
